summary.intent: Implement task #290: the DateRangePicker agnostic core state machine component in a Rust UI component library, including spec, tests, and conformance/proptest entries.
summary.outcome: The DateRangePicker module was fully implemented (82 unit + 28 snapshot + conformance + proptest), the component spec was rewritten to match real dependency APIs, a controlled→uncontrolled consistency bug was discovered and fixed via proptest, and a PR was opened after all xci-fast gates passed.

repo: The date-range-picker spec was written before date_field and range_calendar were finalized, causing pervasive API drift: text-input model with DateFormat/parse_date, calendar{is_range: true} composition, to_iso_string(), and DateRange::normalized() returning a non-Option — all wrong.

date_field is a segmented field emitting Option<CalendarDate>, not a text input. DateFormat does not exist (only DateFormatter). Range selection lives in the dedicated range_calendar component, not calendar{is_range: true}.
CalendarDate::to_iso_string() does not exist; the correct method is to_iso8601(). DateRange::normalized() returns Option, making spec line 340's set(Some(normalized(...))) a type error.
range_calendar::Props impls Default with visible_months: 2, enabling spread-based overrides.
format_date_label differs between range_calendar (appends weekday) and date_range_field — do not merge these into a shared helper.
NoEffect is the correct Effect type for agnostic-core pickers where focus and positioning are adapter concerns.
The snapshot-count gate is a soft per-component budget review cap (not an exact manifest); the workspace-wide total warning is pre-existing and not a per-component failure.
spec-compile-snippets gate performs syntactic Rust parse (syn/rustc token-tree) on all markdown code blocks — placeholder bodies like { /* ... */ } are syntactically valid and pass.
usize fields in ComponentPart-derived Part enums require no #[part(default)] attribute; the derive uses 0.
Adapter specs (Leptos, Dioxus) referenced api.calendar_props() which needed renaming to range_calendar_props() to match the core implementation.

code.finding: sync_props controlled branch must also update the internal value so that a controlled→uncontrolled switch (parent drops value prop) reveals a value consistent with the fields, not a stale None.
Non-comparable mixed-calendar defensive branch — intentionally left uncovered, consistent with date_range_field sibling; only reachable via deliberately mixed CalendarSystem inputs.
date_out_of_bounds and format_date_label helpers promoted to pub(crate) for sibling reuse by date_range_picker, avoiding a third copy.

The field-mirror invariant (value.get() matches start_date/end_date) must be gated on !is_controlled(); in controlled mode get() correctly returns the parent override while fields track edits independently.

workflow: cargo xci-fast is the pre-push gate (~10-15 min): covers fmt, clippy, unit/integration/adapter tests, spec-compile-snippets, error-variant-coverage, mutual-exclusion, snapshot-count, and native coverage.
Run cargo insta review (or cargo insta accept) to generate and accept pending snapshot fixtures after first test run.
Run cargo insta --unreferenced=reject with --all-features to avoid false positives from feature-gated snapshot tests.
Proptest blocks are #[ignore]d (nightly-only) but should be run manually at least once to validate invariants; they can catch real implementation bugs that unit tests miss.

Post-implementation audit phases: (1) spec/impl derive/type drift, (2) adapter spec and surface reference sweep (two clean consecutive rounds), (3) coverage gap-fill. Document uncovered defensive branches explicitly.
SendResult uses state_changed and context_changed fields — there is no transitioned field.
date_field::Props has no From<AttrMap> / Into<AttrMap> impl; return marker attrs for wrapper parts instead of converting Props directly.

friction: Substantial spec drift required extensive research across 5+ files before any implementation could begin — the spec was authoritative in theory but factually wrong in many API details.
SendResult API mismatch (expected .transitioned, actual .state_changed/.context_changed) caused a cascade of test file edits.
date_field::Props having no Into<AttrMap> impl was only discoverable at compile time, requiring a design pivot to marker attrs.
ToString needed an explicit import in the no_std + alloc context even though it's normally in the prelude.
cargo insta --unreferenced=reject produces false positives without --all-features due to feature-gated snapshot tests in other components.
Proptest discovered a real controlled→uncontrolled consistency bug in the initial implementation that required a fix and regression test.
MD060 markdownlint warnings appeared repeatedly during spec editing but are cosmetic (not a CI gate), creating noise.
The two format_date_label implementations look similar but differ in one detail (weekday suffix), making the merge tempting but incorrect.
open_items: Adapter implementations (Leptos, Dioxus) for DateRangePicker are not yet written — adapter specs were updated but no adapter code exists.
Proptest is #[ignore]d and only runs manually or on nightly CI; the invariants it guards are not covered by the always-on suite beyond the named regression test.
E2E tests are deferred — they are an adapter-layer concern and not applicable to the agnostic core.
The non-comparable mixed-calendar defensive branch (mod.rs:872) is intentionally uncovered; a future test could exercise it by constructing deliberately mixed CalendarSystem inputs.
